lede: A look at September 29 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Michelle Bachelet and Lech Wałęsa, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Gol Transportes Aéreos Flight 1907
events.desc: A Boeing 737 and an Embraer 600 collide in mid-air, killing 154 people and triggering a Brazilian aviation crisis.

events.title: John Roberts
events.desc: John Roberts is confirmed as Chief Justice of the United States.

events.title: Lockheed YF-22
events.desc: The YF-22, which would later become the F-22 Raptor, flies for the first time.

events.title: Equatorial Guinea
events.desc: Equatorial Guinean dictator Francisco Macías Nguema is executed by soldiers from Western Sahara.

events.title: Japan
events.desc: Japan establishes diplomatic relations with the People's Republic of China after breaking official ties with the Republic of China.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: During World War II, German forces, with the aid of local Ukrainian collaborators, begin the two-day Babi Yar massacre.

events.title: Mandate for Palestine
events.desc: The Mandate for Palestine takes effect, creating Mandatory Palestine.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: Bulgaria signs the Armistice of Salonica ending its participation.

events.title: Johann Sebastian Bach
events.desc: J. S. Bach leads the first performance of Herr Gott, dich loben alle wir, BWV 130 for the feast of archangel Michael, based on Paul Eber's hymn in twelve stanzas.

events.title: Siege of Canterbury
events.desc: Danes capture Canterbury after a siege, taking Ælfheah, archbishop of Canterbury, as a prisoner.

births.name: Lech Wałęsa
births.line: Polish electrician and politician, 2nd President of Poland, Nobel Prize laureate

births.name: Enrico Fermi
births.line: Italian-American physicist and academic, Nobel Prize laureate

births.name: Silvio Berlusconi
births.line: Italian businessman and politician, Prime Minister of Italy

births.name: Julia Gillard
births.line: Welsh-Australian lawyer and politician, 27th Prime Minister of Australia

births.name: Pier Luigi Bersani
births.line: Italian educator and politician, 6th President of Emilia-Romagna

deaths.text: Kathleen Booth, British computer scientist and mathematician
Rudolf Diesel, German engineer, invented the diesel engine
Émile Zola, French journalist, author, and playwright
Gustav Vasa, King of Sweden from 1523 to 1560

observances.text: Christian feast day: Charles, Duke of Brittany
Christian feast day: the Archangels Michael, Gabriel, and Raphael. One of the four quarter days in the Irish calendar. (England and Ireland). Called Michaelmas in some western liturgical traditions.
